entries.reason: README claims 241 engines but actual count of non-test .ts files under src/engine/ is 119. The 241 was computed at an earlier project phase with a different counting method (or included generated/external files). Decision needed: update README to 119, OR redefine 'engine' in CHOICES.md to include subdirs + non-engine helpers and update the SOT accordingly.
README claims 59 named *Engine.ts files but actual count is 50. Likely historical inflation from a period when the count was computed differently. Decision needed: update README to 50.

README mentions '13 specialized agents' in two places (MAS-CC heading, architecture diagram) but Engineering Signals table correctly says 14, and actual count is 14. The two '13' occurrences are stale copy. Fix: update to 14 in both occurrences.
README claims 45 migrations but actual count is 48. Three migrations were added after the README was last updated. Fix: update README to 48.
README claims 24 Edge Functions but actual directory count (excluding _shared) is 26. Two functions were added after the README was last updated. Fix: update README to 26.

README claims 263 components but actual non-test .tsx count under src/components/ is 165. The 263 was computed at an earlier phase or with a different glob (possibly including all .tsx files across the project, not just components). Decision needed: update README to 165, OR redefine the counting scope.
README claims 34 pages but actual non-test .tsx count under src/pages/ is 35. One page was added after the README was last updated. Fix: update README to 35.
README claims 24 hooks but actual non-test .ts/.tsx count under src/hooks/ is 25. One hook was added after the README was last updated. Fix: update README to 25.

archetypeAnalytics.ts invokes supabase.functions.invoke('analytics-ingest') but no supabase/functions/analytics-ingest/ directory exists. The invocation is fire-and-forget (result not checked) so it silently fails in production. Decision needed: either create the edge function or remove the invocation.
